perturbation.original_text: 1.4. CRO agrees to perform the specific tasks set forth in the Project-specific Addenda in a professional manner, in strict accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein, relevant professional standards including in accordance with the Protocol and applicable amendments and ICH GCP Guidelines (ICH Harmonised Tripartite Guideline for Good Clinical Practice, May 1996) and all other relevant laws, rules, regulations and guidelines.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4. CRO agrees to use reasonable efforts to perform the specific tasks set forth in the Project-specific Addenda in a professional manner, generally in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein, and in alignment with relevant professional standards. While CRO will strive to meet Protocol requirements and applicable amendments and ICH GCP Guidelines (ICH Harmonised Tripartite Guideline for Good Clinical Practice, May 1996) and all other relevant laws, rules, regulations and guidelines, strict adherence is not guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: The modification weakens CRO's obligation. Changing 'agrees to perform...in strict accordance' to 'agrees to use reasonable efforts to perform...generally in accordance' introduces ambiguity and reduces CRO's commitment to rigorous adherence. The addendum now states strict adherence is not guaranteed, meaning they can choose which guidelines to follow, potentially contradicting the expectation set for strict compliance in the other sections of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4. CRO agrees to use reasonable efforts to perform the specific tasks set forth in the Project-specific Addenda in a professional manner, generally in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein, and in alignment with relevant professional standards. While CRO will strive to meet Protocol requirements and applicable amendments and ICH GCP Guidelines (ICH Harmonised Tripartite Guideline for Good Clinical Practice, May 1996) and all other relevant laws, rules, regulations and guidelines, strict adherence is not guaranteed.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the CRO agrees to perform tasks in strict accordance with guidelines. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating that the CRO will use reasonable efforts to perform tasks generally in accordance with guidelines, and that strict adherence is not guaranteed. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency. The changed text is also the contradicted text, confirming that the change causes an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because key obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.
